title: sandbox.config.json
description: Per-repository sandbox configuration. Lives at the repo root; read on the host by sandbox-start (local mode). A partial override of the defaults below — omit any field to accept its default.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema, enabling editor autocomplete + validation.

stack.description: Which golden-<stack> image to launch (toolchain baked in). Equivalent to --stack.

tools.description: mise-managed runtime version overrides, applied at sandbox-start. DEFAULT {} = use the image's pre-warmed latest-LTS (instant, no download). List a tool only to deviate, e.g. {"node": "22"}. Values are mise version specifiers: "lts", "latest", or an explicit version. Works for any tool with a mise core plugin (node, python, go, ...).

cpu.description: CPU limit passed to incus (e.g. "4"). Integer count or Incus cpu range.

egress.description: Domain-based egress filtering. additive to the bundled baseline allowlist (Anthropic + git hosts + package registries + mise/nodejs download hosts).

restrict.description: Turn domain filtering on. Equivalent to --restrict-domains. When false, all HTTPS is open (the port-level egress floor still applies).

allow.description: Extra domains to splice through, ADDED to the bundled baseline. ".example.com" matches all subdomains; "exact.com" matches only that host.

grants.description: What the sandbox is granted access to.

env.description: Names of environment variables to forward into the container. Values are NEVER stored here — they come from the host environment / ~/.sandbox/env. Equivalent to --env (names only).

sshAgent.description: Run the deploy-key SSH agent in the container (enables git push-back).

codexLogin.description: Forward container port 1455 to the same host port, so `sandbox <name> --codex` can complete Codex's browser OAuth login (which calls back to localhost:1455) from inside the cage.

screen.description: In-cage dependency screening (shadowed npm/pnpm/yarn/npx via screen-node). Equivalent to the inverse of --no-screen.
